contraventions.description: A licensee must not employ a person in a community care facility unless the licensee or, in the case of a person who is not the manager, the manager has first met with the person and obtained all of the following: a criminal record check for the person
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake March 30, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that criminal record checks had not been obtained for staff that were permitted to work with children.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that the policies and procedures referred to in subsection (1) are implemented by employees.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake March 30, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined staff members did not conduct a head count before leaving the outdoor area as outlined in their care and supervision policy, leaving two children without supervision.

contraventions.description: Without limiting subsection (1), a licensee must ensure that the ratio of employees to children attending a community care facility is no less than that permitted in Schedule E.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake March 30, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the Group Child Care (Under 36 Months) and Group Child Care (30 Months to School Age) programs did not meet the staff to child ratios permitted under Schedule E of the Child Care Licensing Regulation in the mornings. Multiple programs, including Dolphin, Sealion, Starfish, Jellyfish, Octopus, and Salmon were over ratio between March 2 and March 10, 2026.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that children are supervised at all times by a person who is an educator, an assistant or a responsible adult.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake March 30, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that on March 25, 2026, two children were left in the outdoor area without supervision.

contraventions.description: A licensee must do all of the following:(b) operate the community care facility in a manner that will promote the health, safety and dignity of persons in care
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake March 30, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the terms and conditions of the licence were not followed, as the Group Child Care (Under 36 Months) programs frequently exceeded the licensed capacity during morning hours.

contraventions.description: A licensee must do all of the following:(b) operate the community care facility in a manner that will promote the health, safety and dignity of persons in care
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake October 7, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that staff did not demonstrate skills to promote health, safety and dignity of children in care when they did not respond promptly to children's severe illness symptoms, follow and implemented procedures and policies, notify parents and Licensing of reportable incidents.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that the policies and procedures referred to in subsection (1) are implemented by employees.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake October 7, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that policies and procedures in place were not implemented by staff.

contraventions.description: A licensee must immediately notify a parent or emergency contact if, while under the care or supervision of the licensee, the child becomes ill or is injured,
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake October 7, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that a child became ill while in care and parent was not notified immediately.

contraventions.description: A licensee must notify the medical health officer within 24 hours after (a) a child is involved in, or may have been involved in, a reportable incident described in Schedule H while under the care or supervision of the licensee,
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake October 7, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that a reportable incident was not notified to the Medical Health Officer within 24 hours.

contraventions.description: A licensee must not employ a person in a community care facility unless the licensee or, in the case of a person who is not the manager, the manager has first met with the person and obtained all of the following: a criminal record check for the person
contraventions.observations: Licensing audited 20 staff files and one file did not contain a criminal record check.

contraventions.description: A licensee must not employ a person in a community care facility unless the licensee or, in the case of a person who is not the manager, the manager has first met with the person and obtained all of the following: evidence that the person has complied with the Province’s immunization and tuberculosis control programs
contraventions.observations: Licensing audited nine staff files. Two files did not contain the immunization status.

contraventions.description: A licensee must keep, for each child, a record showing the following information: (a)name, sex, date of birth, medical insurance plan number (MSP number does not apply to REC only) and immunization status.
contraventions.observations: Licensing audited eight children's files. One record did not contain the sex of the child.

contraventions.description: A licensee must keep, for each child, a record showing the following information: (d) name and telephone number of a parent, medical practitioner (doctor's name and number does not apply to REC only) and emergency contact
contraventions.observations: Licensing audited eight children's files. One file did not contain a record of the name and telephone number of the medical partitioner and the emergency contact.

contraventions.description: A licensee must have in writing from a parent, and maintain at the community care facility, consent (a) to call a medical practitioner or ambulance in case of accident or illness if the parent cannot immediately be reached,
contraventions.observations: Licensing audited eight children's files. One file did not contain a written consent from parent to call a medical practitioner or ambulance in case of accident or illness if parent cannot immediately be reached.

contraventions.observations: Regarding intake February 2, 2026. [Child A] was located in a washroom on their own. [Child A] was brought to a staff and returned to care. The Licensee did not immediately notify a parent or emergency contact of the incident where a child was missing or wandering.

contraventions.description: A licensee must notify the medical health officer within 24 hours after (a) a child is involved in, or may have been involved in, a reportable incident described in Schedule H while under the care or supervision of the licensee,

contraventions.description: A licensee must keep current records of each of the following: (f) a log of minor accidents, illnesses and unexpected events involving children, that did not require medical attention and were not reportable incidents described in Schedule H.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake January 19, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that staff had observed [child A] push, kick, and hit other children. Staff verbally informed parents of the minor incidents [child A] was involved in. While the incidents did not meet the criteria for submitting a reportable incident, the Licensee was required to maintain records of all minor accidents, illnesses and unexpected events that involved [child A]. Licensing did not observe any associated documentation at the facility that [child A] had pushed, kicked, and hit [unexpected events].

contraventions.description: A licensee must do all of the following:(b) operate the community care facility in a manner that will promote the health, safety and dignity of persons in care
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake December 1, 2025, through the investigation process, Licensing determined that a staff member behaved disrespectfully toward coworkers on multiple occasions in the presence of children. Licensing confirmed two specific incidents that occurred. One was in the gym (exact date not recalled by staff), and another occurred on December 8, 2025, during which a staff member raised their voice and rolled their eyes at colleagues. The information gathered was consistent across sources and demonstrated conduct that was inappropriate and unprofessional in the child care setting.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that behavioural guidance is appropriate to the age and development of the child who is receiving the guidance,
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake December 1, 2025, through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the facility had a policy requiring staff to communicate clearly and set limits with children in a positive manner. Licensing found that a staff member did not set limits in a positive way. The staff member self-reported that they used a 'bear hug' to move a child as a behavioural guidance technique. Using a 'bear hug' to move a child is not consistent with positive guidance or the facility's policy.
